claims.metric: Soft vs binary toxicity detection AUROC across base rates
claims.value: Soft AUROC = 1.000 ± 0.000 across 0.05–0.50 adversarial base rates; binary AUROC = 0.91–0.96
claims.evidence: 100 runs (5 base rates × 10 seeds × 2 replication runs), 40-agent populations, 24 epochs, Bonferroni-corrected

claims.value: Soft median TTD 0.28 epochs (100% detection) vs binary 9.93 epochs (88% detection) — 35× faster at FPR ≤ 0.05
claims.evidence: 20 paired runs across 2 replication studies, 40 agents/run, paired Wilcoxon p < 1e-30, Holm-Bonferroni significant

claims.metric: Net social welfare under proxy calibration drift
claims.value: Cumulative-toxicity-driven sigmoid degradation causes deterministic welfare collapse: +2.87 → −1.54 (Δ = −4.41), the only dynamic-toxicity mechanism producing negative net welfare
claims.evidence: 42 seeds, dynamic-toxicity comparison across 6 feedback mechanisms, drift α=0.3, 40% adversarial, 30 epochs, Bonferroni-corrected

claims.metric: Long-horizon screening effect size, 10-seed vs 50-seed estimate
claims.value: Tight-governance effect deflates d = 2.75 → 0.38 (n.s.) under replication; moderate-governance effect d = 0.76 (p = 0.0001, Bonferroni-significant) survives
claims.evidence: 10-seed confirmatory arm (20260326_screening_strength_sweep) vs 50-seed focused replication (20260328_long_horizon_screening_replication), α/12 = 0.004

claims.metric: Capability loss from oracle to tight governance, by task type
claims.value: Allocation loses 10.4% (d = 6.63); coordination 29.0% (d = 6.08); routing 37.2% (d = 1.34); long-horizon 45.7% (d = 1.63) — governance cost scales with task coordination demand
claims.evidence: 50 seeds × 4 task types × 7 governance regimes, all comparisons Bonferroni-significant
